## reference · localization
* Localization (l10n) is the process of adapting a software user interface to a specific culture.
* Consider the following factors when localizing:
  * Language
  * Unit of measure (e.g., kilometers in Europe, miles in U.S.)
  * Text direction (e.g., European languages are left-to-right, Arabic right-to-left)
  * Capitalization in Latin script
  * Adaptation of idioms
  * Use of register
  * Number format
  * Date format
  * Currency
  * Cultural references
  * Paper size
  * Color psychology
  * Compliance with local laws
  * Local holidays
  * Personal names
* The complementary practice of making a system easier to localize is called Internationalization.

Sources: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Localization
